ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for steamed pork and vegetable dumplings; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared ethnic dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of steamed pork and vegetable dumplings is approximately 6 pieces (about 150 g), suitable as a main dish for one person or a shared starter for two.

nutrient_source.title: Dumplings, steamed, pork and vegetable — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for steamed pork and vegetable dumplings (cr_id G00010299). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are preserved from the HPB record. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for pork and vegetable dumplings (FDC ID 2103688) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; dietary fibre estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest match.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — steamed wheat-flour dumplings (jiaozi) analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for 'Dumpling Party For 4 Persons' exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 48 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: steamed wheat-flour filled dumplings (jiaozi/dim sum category), which report GI values in the range 45–52 in peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021). GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 12.05) / 100 ≈ 5.8 for a 150 g serving (12.05 g carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium at 46 mg/100 g with supportive potassium at 82 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Steamed pork and vegetable dumplings are suitable for individuals with hypertension when consumed plain; however, dipping sauces such as soy sauce or chili sauce can substantially increase sodium intake and should be used sparingly or replaced with reduced-sodium alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (48), low carbohydrate (8 g/100 g), negligible sugar (0.21 g/100 g), and high protein (8.71 g/100 g) moderating postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Steamed dumplings have a low glycemic index and modest carbohydrate load, making them a reasonable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion control is advised as wheat flour wrappers contribute refined carbohydrates, and sweet dipping sauces should be avoided to prevent added sugar load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.2 g/100 g), low sodium (46 mg/100 g), and absence of trans fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Steamed preparation avoids added fats, and the estimated saturated fat content of approximately 1.2 g/100 g is well below the threshold of concern; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and avoid high-sodium dipping sauces to maintain a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork filling contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6), which may amplify pro-inflammatory pathways at high intake
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Anti-inflammatory ingredients including ginger, garlic, and cabbage are beneficial; however, individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit portion size to manage omega-6 arachidonic acid load from the pork filling, and verify actual saturated fat content with the vendor.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed pork and vegetable dumplings (jiǎozi; 饺子) are a classic Chinese dish consisting of seasoned ground pork mixed with finely chopped cabbage, ginger, garlic, spring onion, sesame oil, and soy sauce, enclosed in thin wheat flour wrappers and cooked by steaming. The result is a tender, juicy parcel with a delicate wrapper that is slightly chewy and a savoury, aromatic filling. Dumplings are a staple of Chinese cuisine and are widely consumed across Singapore, commonly served at lunch or dinner as a shared dish or main course. Per 100 g, steamed pork and vegetable dumplings provide approximately 101 kcal, 8.71 g protein, 3.78 g fat, and 8.03 g carbohydrate, with very low sodium (46 mg) and negligible sugar (0.21 g). The high protein content and low glycemic index (estimated GI 48) make them a nutritionally balanced option suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when consumed without high-sodium dipping sauces. Iron content is notable at 1.79 mg/100 g. The dish is dairy-free but contains gluten (wheat) and soy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in steamed dumplings?
Are dumplings good for high blood pressure?
What is the GI of dumplings?
Are steamed dumplings low in fat?
Is dumpling suitable for heart patients?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010299, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat and dietary fibre estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest match and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for steamed wheat-flour dumplings with peer-reviewed literature support.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for steamed pork and vegetable dumplings is not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation as of 2026-04-16. 2. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB source for this entry. 3. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond those reported by HPB is not available in the primary source for this dish as of 2026-04-16; USDA FoodData Central does not have a sufficiently close match to provide reliable estimates for all these fields. 4. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: steamed preparation with no hydrogenated fats in the standard recipe; confirmed negligible per USDA FoodData Central pork dumpling entries. 5. saturated_fat_g (1.2 g/100 g) and dietary_fibre_g (1.5 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 2103688 (pork and vegetable dumplings, steamed) as the closest approved-source analogue; HPB does not separately report these fields for this entry. 6. food_subgroup updated from 'Baking ingredients' (input) to 'Stuffed dough / dumpling products' as the original subgroup was clearly misclassified. 7. The input record name 'Dumpling Party For 4 Persons' is a menu/commercial label; canonical food name normalised to 'Steamed Pork and Vegetable Dumplings' for database integrity. 8. rheumatoid_arthritis health context preserved from input and placed under additional_conditions.

improvement_actions_taken: Normalised food_name from menu label 'Dumpling Party For 4 Persons' to canonical 'Steamed Pork and Vegetable Dumplings'.
Added Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names to satisfy G6 multilingual requirement for Chinese/Singaporean dish.
Added Japanese alias (餃子) given ethnic_tags include Japanese in input.
Estimated saturated_fat_g (1.2) and dietary_fibre_g (1.5) from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 2103688 with full documentation.
Added GI (48, low), gi_category, glycemic_load_per_serving (5.8), and gi_source from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate.
Added health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with clinical guidance.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes per G3 requirement.
Corrected food_subgroup from 'Baking ingredients' to 'Stuffed dough / dumpling products'.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency (q8 self-check passed).
Added ontology anchors (FoodOn, FoodEx2) with honest SNOMED CT gap note.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
